person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Father Sir Richard de Fourneux

Mother Sibella b. c 1270

Joan de Fourneux was born at of Carlton in Lindrick, Nottinghamshire, England.

She married Sir John Orreby, 1st Lord Orreby, son of Fulke de Orreby, Chief Justice of Chester and (Miss) Strange, before June 1317.

Joan de Fourneux married Sir Henry FitzHenry, Lord FitzHugh, son of Sir Henry FitzHugh, Constable of Barnard Castle and Eve de Bulmer, between March 1330 and September 1331.

They had 1 son (Henry, 2nd Lord FitzHugh) & 1 daughter (Joan, wife of William Greystoke, 2nd Lord Greystoke, Lord FitzWilliam, of Sir Anthony de Lucy, 3rd Lord Lucy, & of Sir Matthew Redman).

Joan de Fourneux died on 21 September 1349.

«b»Family 1«/b»
Sir Henry FitzHenry, Lord FitzHugh d. 24 Sep 1352

«b»Children«/b»
1.) Hugh FitzHenry d. b 21 Sep 1349

2.) Joan FitzHenry d. 1 Sep 1403

3.) Henry FitzHugh, 2nd Lord FitzHugh b. c 1338, d. 29 Aug 1386

«b»Family 2«/b»
Sir John Orreby, 1st Lord Orreby d. c 18 Mar 1329

In 5 Edward III [25 January 1331-24 January 1332], at Chester, Henry Fitzhenry and Joan his wife, late wife of John de Orreby, sued for dower:

Roger son of Robert le Bruyn, of Stapelford, eight acres of pasture, six acres of wood, and three acres of moor in Foukestapelford;
John son of Nicholas de Huxlegh, three acres and a half of meadow and two acres of moor in Foukestapelford;
Robert son of William de Huxlegh, one messuage and eight acres of land in Foukestapelford;
John son of William Cocus, three messuages and fifteen acres of land in Foukestapelford;
William son of William de Rosmegrene, one messuage and six and a half acres of land Foukestapelford;
William de Wetfeld, five messuages and twelve acres of land in Foukestapelford; and
Ellen who was the wife of Richard de Fouleshurst, three messuages, one water-mill, fifty-six acres of land, six acres of meadow, and three acres of wood in Wich-Malbank and Batynton.

The said Roger, John, Robert, John, William, William, and Ellen vouch to warranty John son of Philip de Orreby kinsman and heir of John de Orreby.

zh.lifeSummary: Joan Fourneux是Richard Fourneux ( sir)和Sibil of Northampton Fourneux的女兒，出生在英國諾廷加姆爾卡爾頓 在 林德利可。她於大約1324年與Henry FitzHugh在英國越可爾拉文斯沃斯結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1349年9月21日在英國英格蘭，埋葬在英國越可爾易亞斯特 維頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Joan Fourneux是Richard Fourneux ( sir)和Sibil of Northampton Fourneux的女儿，出生在英国諾廷加姆爾卡爾頓 在 林德利可。她于大约1324年与Henry FitzHugh在英国越可爾拉文斯沃斯结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1349年9月21日在英国英格兰，埋葬在英国越可爾易亞斯特 維頓。

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: Joan Fourneux was born in Carlton in Lindrick, Nottinghamshire, England as the daughter of Richard Fourneux ( sir) and Sibil of Northampton Fourneux. She married Henry FitzHugh about 1324, in Ravensworth, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died on 21 September 1349, in England, United Kingdom, and was buried in East Witton, Yorkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Joan Fourneux est née à Carlton in Lindrick, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, fille de Richard Fourneux ( sir) et Sibil of Northampton Fourneux. Elle a épousé Henry FitzHugh environ 1324, à Ravensworth, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 21 septembre 1349, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, et a été enterrée à East Witton, Everwyck, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Joan Fourneux wurde in Carlton in Lindrick, Nottinghamshire, England, als Tochter von Richard Fourneux ( sir) und Sibil of Northampton Fourneux geboren. Sie hat um 1324 in Ravensworth, Yorkshire, England Henry FitzHugh geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 21. September 1349 in England, United Kingdom und wurde in East Witton, Yorkshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Joan Fourneux è nata il nel Carlton in Lindrick, Nottinghamshire, England come figlia di Richard Fourneux ( sir) e Sibil of Northampton Fourneux. Lei sposato Henry FitzHugh circa 1324, nel Ravensworth, Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì il 21 settembre 1349, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito, e fu sepolta a East Witton, Yorkshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Fourneuxはイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアカールトン イン リンドリックでRichard Fourneux ( sir)とSibil of Northampton Fourneuxの娘として生まれた。約1324年にイギリスヨークシュアイアラベンスウオルスでHenry FitzHughと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1349年9月21日にイギリスイギリスで死去し，イギリスヨークシュアイアエアスト ウィットオンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Fourneux(은)는 영국 노틴그함실르 칼턴 인 린드릭에서 Richard Fourneux ( sir)(와)과 Sibil of Northampton Fourneux의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1324년 영국 요르크실르 레이븐스워스에서 Henry FitzHugh(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1349년 9월 21일 영국 잉글랜드에서 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 이스트 윗튼에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Joan Fourneux nasceu em Carlton in Lindrick, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra filha de Richard Fourneux ( sir) e Sibil of Northampton Fourneux. Ela casou-se com Henry FitzHugh aproximadamente 1324, em Ravensworth, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 21 de setembro de 1349, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom, e foi sepultada em East Witton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Joan Fourneux родилась в местности карлтон ин линдрикк, ноттингхамшир, Англия. Её родителей звали Richard Fourneux ( sir) и Sibil of Northampton Fourneux. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Henry FitzHugh около 1324 г. в местности равенсворт, еркшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла 21 сентября 1349 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания и была похоронена в местности эаст виттон, еркшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Joan Fourneux nació en Carlton in Lindrick, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra como hija de Richard Fourneux ( sir) y Sibil of Northampton Fourneux. Se casó con Henry FitzHugh aproximadamente 1324, en Ravensworth, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió el 21 de septiembre de 1349, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido, y fue sepultada en East Witton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.
